data.title: Se7en Interiors Inc. Announces Release of Framework on Wellness-Oriented Cottage Environments and Natural Interior Design
data.description:
Orillia, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - February 20, 2026) - Se7en Interiors Inc. announces release of a framework addressing wellness-oriented residential environments in cottage properties and the role of natural interior design in market perception. The announcement presents compiled observations regarding buyer attention to environments characterized by material authenticity, sensory comfort, and visual continuity with surrounding landscapes. The framework documents design conditions associated with retreat-oriented living environments and interior configurations aligned with restorative use patterns.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/9405/284649_89d751eee4cf7843_001full.jpg

The released framework outlines contextual factors associated with a measurable price premium for properties that thoughtfully integrate natural and sustainable design within vacation homes located in lakefront and forest-adjacent settings. Documented considerations include material sourcing transparency, use of organic textures, spatial planning aligned with views and daylight, and interior environments structured around restorative experiences. Content describes interior environments as spatial systems shaped by material origin awareness, tactile variation, and visual integration with terrain characteristics.

Framework content describes current market attention to natural interior design as a design approach emphasizing material provenance, durability, and environmental context. The framework references broader market indicators, including projections that the global sustainable home decor market is anticipated to expand to $663.9 billion by 2034. Documented material reviews examine wood species with visible grain structure, stone applications exhibiting geological variation, glazing configurations oriented toward landscape visibility, and textile selections emphasizing natural fibers and layered textures.

The framework presents analysis of interior conditions observed in residential environments prepared for market listing within retreat property contexts. Observational findings address relationships among interior atmosphere, perceived tranquility, and buyer evaluation patterns. Environmental integration is described through alignment of architectural features, interior finishes, and exterior landscape conditions. Spatial sequencing analysis evaluates transitions between entry zones, gathering areas, and private rooms in relation to daylight exposure and view corridors.

Methodology described within the framework includes site context review, material evaluation protocols, spatial cohesion assessment, and documentation of environmental references present within interior compositions. Analytical structure organizes interior variables into categories addressing material composition, sensory environment, and landscape continuity. Documentation emphasizes unified design planning in relation to property identity and site characteristics rather than discrete decorative interventions.

Se7en Interiors Inc. reports ongoing application of region-specific design analysis within cottage environments characterized by granite formations, coniferous vegetation, and waterfront exposure. Released material documents professional planning processes examining cohesion among architectural elements, interior assemblies, and exterior site conditions within residential environments associated with seasonal occupancy patterns. Research activity described in the framework examines relationships among interior environment configuration, buyer perception, and property presentation strategies within retreat property markets.

The framework release forms part of continuing documentation examining environmental integration within residential interiors and associated market observations across cottage property settings.

About Se7en Interiors Inc.

Se7en Interiors Inc. is an interior design practice based in Ontario, Canada. The firm provides residential design planning, material selection consultation, and spatial development services for cottage and residential properties. Project work includes concept development, technical documentation, and coordination with local craftspeople and trades.

MEDIA DETAIL

Contact Person Name: Kaitlyn Taylor (Owner & Lead Designer)

Company Name: Se7en Interiors Inc.

Email: info@se7eninteriors.com

Website: https://se7eninteriors.com/

Phone: 289-552-1221

Street Address: 3 Progress Dr Unit 11

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/284649

data.title: Gold rises on US-Iran tensions and lower European bond yields
data.description:
Key points:
  • US sets 15-day deadline for Iran to sign nuclear deal
  • Silver, platinum and palladium set for weekly gains
  • US inflation data due at 1330 GMT

By Pablo Sinha

Gold prices rose on Friday, supported by lingering tensions between the United States and Iran and lower European bond yields while investors awaited U.S. inflation data to assess the outlook for Federal Reserve monetary policy.

Spot gold was up 0.7% at $5,032.49 an ounce by 0941 GMT, but was on course for a weekly decline of 0.2%. U.S. gold futures for April delivery were up 1.1% at $5,052.70.

"What we're seeing in the interest rate markets is that prices are higher in the longer end in European trading. That means yields are down, and that is reducing opportunity costs for holding gold," said Quantitative Commodity Research analyst Peter Fertig.

Euro zone government bond yields were set for a second straight weekly decline on Friday in a week marked by speculation over the leadership of the European Central Bank and by rising geopolitical tensions.

U.S. President Donald Trump warned Iran on Thursday that it must make a deal over its nuclear programme or "really bad things" will happen, setting a deadline of 10 to 15 days.

Goldman Sachs said in a note that in its base case, with central bank buying re-accelerating and private investors adding exposure only in response to U.S. interest rate cuts, it expects gold to grind slowly higher this year.

U.S. personal consumption expenditure data, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, is in focus for clues on U.S. monetary policy. Economists polled by Reuters forecast a 0.3% rise in core PCE. The data is due at 1330 GMT.

Traders largely expect the central bank to hold interest rates steady at its March policy meeting, according to CME's FedWatch Tool.

Non-yielding bullion tends to perform well when interest rates are low.

Gold demand remained subdued in India this week as volatile prices deterred buyers while other major Asian hubs, including China, were closed for the Lunar New Year holidays.

Elsewhere, spot silver gained 2.8% to $80.57 an ounce. Spot platinum rose 2.3% to $2,117.66 while palladium added 2.3% to $1,723.25. All metals were poised to register weekly gains.

data.title: Rule changes boost retail bond market access, says NSDL CEO
data.description:
India’s retail bond market is finally expanding, and according to Vijay Chandok, MD & CEO of National Securities Depository Limited (NSDL), the real trigger is not investor awareness but regulation that made bonds investable.

Explaining what changed structurally, Chandok said the turning point was the introduction of Online Bond Platform Provider (OBPP) rules. On the key catalyst, he said, “The real, compelling regulation… which has really propelled the bond market forward is the OBPP regulations.” The framework has effectively opened access to bonds, much as online platforms earlier widened participation in equities and mutual funds.

The impact is already visible in distribution.

On platform growth, Chandok stated, “We have more than 40 of them in the country now,” adding that NSDL has partnered with many of these platforms to provide digital onboarding and simplified processes for investors. He said the industry has moved toward “facilitation, paperlessness, digitalisation, information flow,” which is pushing retail participation higher.

Another major hurdle earlier was the lack of transparency and investor understanding. NSDL is attempting to solve this through information access.

On investor education, Chandok said the depository provides “a plethora of information about bonds… which really helps a retail investor to understand the risks and then take an informed decision,” including yield and pricing data through its trade repository.

Lower entry barriers are also bringing bonds closer to a mass retail product. Chandok pointed to SEBI’s recent policy move.

On accessibility, he said, “SEBI has brought down the minimum investment from ₹100,000 to ₹10,000. I think that's a very positive step.” Along with growing advisor participation, bonds are increasingly being positioned as a diversification tool for retail portfolios.

He believes the market is now beginning to follow the adoption path once seen in mutual funds — digital platforms, advisors, and smaller-ticket sizes. “We are beginning to see some components of this coming to the bond market as well,” he said, adding that a conducive regulatory and interest-rate environment is supporting participation.

Despite the progress, India’s bond market remains small compared to bank financing and global peers, though it is steadily expanding.

On issuance growth, Chandok said, “Two years back, we saw the total issuances of about nine lakh crore… this year it's likely to be eleven.” He stressed that further expansion will depend on investor education, stating bond concepts are not easy for first-time investors and “explanation is important” for wider adoption.

Catch all the latest updates from the stock market here

data.title: 'Borderline hoarder': Tori Spelling says her $12,000-a-month rental where she lives with her 5 kids is a mess
data.description:
By Kelsi Karruli

Her podcast co-host criticizes Spelling's lifestyle.

Tori Spelling attends an iHeartRadio event in Los Angeles Dec. 5, 2025.

TV personality Tori Spelling has admitted that she is living like a "borderline hoarder," confessing that she no longer feels comfortable having people visit her lavish California rental home because it has fallen into such a state of disarray.

The 52-year-old actress opened up about the falling standards at her property-which is understood to be located in Calabasas and costs Spelling around $12,000 a month-during a new appearance on her iHeartRadio podcast, "misSpelling."

Returning to the show after a two-month hiatus, the "Beverly Hills, 90210" star explained that she had taken time off to deal with a spate of illnesses, revealing that one particularly bad bout had left her unable to work for eight weeks.

"I know I have had a past of getting sick a lot and frequently, but this one took me down eight weeks. I couldn't work," she said.

Spelling blamed her five children for the outbreak at her property, joking: "I mean it started with the five petri dishes that live in my house, but they bring things home, that's normal. And, you know, you work through it, you get sick."

However, her co-host, Amy Sugarman, claimed there may be another factor to blame for her ongoing health issues.

"[Your] lifestyle contributes to why you get sick so much," she said, hinting that Spelling's surroundings may also be playing a role in her illnesses.

To prove that point, Sugarman asked Spelling to send her a photo of the current state of her home, then exclaiming, "Oh, it's not good," when she saw the image.

"I don't want to be mean, but ... the Christmas tree is up," Sugarman continued, looking at the snap.

"Damn," Spelling replied, "I thought I cut that out!"

Her co-host, however, refused to back down on her critique of the state of Spelling's home, insisting that there is plenty of potential within the dwelling but that it is hard to see for all the mess.

"You can see the bones in here, like the shelves could be really pretty; the living room could be really pretty. You have beautiful things. You can't even sit in there," she said.

"It's borderline hoarder, I'm not going to lie," Sugarman added, as Spelling was forced to agree.

Tori Spelling in 2024.

The barrage didn't end there, with the co-host telling Spelling that while her own home looks like "two sacks of s-t," the Hollywood heiress' property looks like it has "400 sacks of s-t in [it] right now."

Despite agreeing with much of Sugarman's comments, Spelling did attempt to defend herself, noting that, while her living room may be a mess, the kitchen-which is where everyone typically "hangs and eats"-is clean.

Still, Spelling appeared somewhat mortified by the state of her property, admitting that she has even stopped having guests over to the property because it's so messy, noting that the policy even applies to the iHeart Radio crews who record her show.

When asked by Sugarman whether she would consent to hosting the president of the radio network, Spelling responded: "I would have to pass. I'd be like, 'Where? I'll come to you wherever you are.' I would change everything. I stopped having people over."

More from Realtor.com: Phantom Humming Plagues Connecticut Town-Drawing Hundreds of Complaints

The A-lister noted that her home wasn't always a mess, explaining that when she was with her ex-husband, Dean McDermott, he would keep their dwelling clean.

"When Dean and I were together and lived together, I was able to have a house that was presentable because he wouldn't live like that. We were working at different times, so I had downtime when he'd work," she said.

Spelling further insisted that if someone helped her organize it, she would be able to maintain it.

"My busy, chaotic work and children lifestyle is not going to change. In fact, in the next year, there's going to be things coming up, it's going to be escalated. So I need to be on my A-game, and once someone comes in and does all this, yes, I can maintain it if I have someone helping me," she said.

More from Realtor.com: Kelly Clarkson Reveals She's Ending Her NBC Talk Show To 'Prioritize' Her Kids-so, Will the TV Host Leave New York for Good?

Spelling previously questioned if she was a "hoarder" during a candid conversation with Robin Zasio, psychologist from the show "Hoarders."

She also revealed she had 50 storage units the size of "shipping containers" as well as a bill of $80,000. She ultimately had to sell the items inside to pay the bill.

More from Realtor.com: Done Waiting: Citizens Push To Abolish Property Taxes, State by State

Spelling reportedly resides in a $12,000-a-month rental in Calabasas with her five children: Liam, 18, Stella, 17, Hattie, 14, Finn, 13, and Beau, 8.

In 2023, Spelling made headlines when it was revealed that she lived in an RV following a severe mold issue in her previous rental, which caused chronic illnesses.

Spelling is most famous for her role as Donna Martin on "Beverly Hills, 90210," a TV series produced by her father, Aaron Spelling. She's also appeared in "Scary Movie 2," "Trick," and "House of Yes."

The actress was raised in a spectacular Holmby Hills estate that was custom-built for her parents and became widely known as Spelling Manor.

More from Realtor.com: Trump Releases First Rendering of the Planned White House Ballroom

Aaron and his wife, Candy Spelling, built the sprawling estate in 1990 and lived there for more than 20 years. After Aaron died, Candy placed the home on the market for the sky-high price of $150 million. It was eventually sold for $85 million in 2011 to Formula 1 heiress Petra Ecclestone.

This story originally ran on Realtor.com.

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

19 Feb 2026

data.title: EPAM Systems Is Maintained at Buy by Needham

data.title: Slovenia Unemployment Rate at 10-Month High
data.description:
Slovenia’s unemployment rate rose to 4.8% in December 2025 from 4.6% in the previous two months, marking the highest level since February.

The number of unemployed persons increased by 1,381 month-on-month to 47,177.

Unemployed men totaled 24,574, while women accounted for 22,603.

By age group, unemployment stood at 7.7% for those aged 15–29, 4.8% for those aged 50 and older, and 5.8% for those aged 55 and above.

For comparison, the jobless rate was 4.8% in December of the previous year.

Meanwhile, employers reported 9,489 job vacancies, down 3.3% year-on-year, and 157,803 vacancies for the full year, up slightly from 157,384 in the same period last year.

data.title: Anglo American hit by De Beers writedown, posts $3.7 billion loss
data.description:
Key points:
  • Mining group advancing plans to shed non-core assets
  • Company declares dividend of $0.23 a share
  • Core earnings of $6.4 billion in line with analysts' estimate

By Clara Denina

Anglo American posted a $3.7 billion loss on Friday after taking another writedown on its diamonds business, as the miner pushes ahead with plans to shed non-core assets and complete its merger with Teck Resources .

Anglo wrapped up a mixed reporting season for London-listed mining groups, underscoring the industry's diverging fortunes as Antofagasta benefited from soaring copper prices while diversified peers struggled with weaker iron ore, diamond and coal markets.

The company booked a $2.3 billion pre-tax impairment tied to its De Beers unit, reducing the carrying value to $2.3 billion from more than $4 billion previously.

Core earnings, or EBITDA, of $6.4 billion were in line with an analysts' estimate. It declared a dividend of $0.23 per share, or about $200 million. That was down from $0.64 a share, or $800 million, a year earlier.

The company's share price was up 1.7% by 0919 GMT.

Anglo, which in July discontinued nickel and steelmaking coal assets that it is seeking to sell, aims to focus on copper and iron ore assets.

It demerged its platinum business in May, and said it is moving forward with plans to sell De Beers.

The company announced a potential partnership with Mitsubishi Corp for its Woodsmith fertiliser project in northern England, which it had placed on care and maintenance.

"We believe this potential partnership would add optionality and time to pursue further syndication/partnerships," said Goldman Sachs analysts.

DE BEERS SPIN-OFF

Anglo has reassessed the value of De Beers after the diamonds unit posted a third consecutive annual drop in production. It also cut De Beers' 2026 production forecast as weak demand and high inventories continue to weigh on the diamond market.

Anglo had already written down De Beers' value by about $3.5 billion over the past two years. "There is at the moment a plentiful supply of rough diamonds in the market," CEO Duncan Wanblad told reporters.

The sale of De Beers is at an advanced stage, he said. "We need to... get to final binding bids and then pick the partner that we want to go forward with and negotiate that with all the parties involved, including the government of Botswana," he added.

De Beers, which Anglo put up for sale as part of a broader restructuring, has drawn interest from multiple consortia, Wanblad said.

Botswana, already a 15% shareholder and source of 70% of its annual rough diamond production, has said it intends to increase its stake.

Angola is pursuing a 20-30% stake in De Beers, a proposal under discussion with other African diamond producers, a senior official from Angola's mining ministry told Reuters earlier this month.

Wanblad said he is "optimistic" that a deal will be signed this year.

TECK TIE-UP

As large-scale mining consolidation remains elusive despite pressure on companies to expand their copper portfolios, Anglo is the only major miner to have secured a deal, announcing in September a $53 billion all-stock, nil-premium merger with Teck.

On Friday, Wanblad said he expects the deal to be approved some time between September and March, as it awaits China and South Korea's regulatory approvals.

The deal, which would create the world's fifth-largest copper producer, proceeded even as the world's largest miner BHP Group attempted to take over Anglo.

The combined entity is projected to produce more than 1.2 million metric tons of copper annually.

Copper, a key metal for the power and construction industries, is poised to benefit from surging demand driven by electric vehicles and artificial intelligence.
